6.9, "vector_string": "CVSS:4.0/AV:N/AC:L/AT:N/PR:N/UI:N/VC:L/VI:N/VA:N/SC:N/SI:N/SA:N"}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-209", "name": "Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information"}], "description": "### Impact

Parse Server's GraphQL endpoint discloses schema metadata to unauthenticated callers through `Did you mean ...?` suggestions embedded in GraphQL validation-error messages. An unauthenticated caller who knows only the public application id can iteratively send malformed queries to reconstruct class names, field names, argument names, mutation names, and input-object fields. This bypasses the `IntrospectionControlPlugin` enforced when `graphQLPublicIntrospection: false` (the default) and defeats the schema-hiding goal of prior advisories GHSA-48q3-prgv-gm4w and GHSA-q5q9-2rhp-33qw. Schema disclosure aids reconnaissance for downstream authorization probing but does not by itself leak object data or authentication material.

### Patches

A new `SchemaSuggestionsControlPlugin` Apollo plugin strips the `Did you mean ...?` suffix from GraphQL validation-error messages during `validationDidStart`, which runs before any introspection gate. The plugin applies only when `graphQLPublicIntrospection: false` and the caller is not a master-key or maintenance-key holder, matching the trust model of the existing `IntrospectionControlPlugin`.

### Workarounds

No code workaround is available short of disabling the GraphQL API (`mountGraphQL: false`). Operators who require disclosure-resistant validation errors should upgrade to a patched release.

### Resources

- GitHub security advisory: https://github.com/parse-community/parse-server/security/advisories/GHSA-8cph-rgr4-g5vj
- Fix Parse Server 9: https://github.com/parse-community/parse-server/pull/10467
- Fix Parse Server 8: https://github.com/parse-community/parse-server/pull/10468", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00291, "percentile": 0.20621}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-8cph-rgr4-g5vj", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-05-29T19:18:01Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-8cph-rgr4-g5vj", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-8cph-rgr4-g5vj"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-47248"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-06-12T19:16:28Z", "published_at": "2026-05-29T19:18:01Z", "references": ["https://github.com/parse-community/parse-server/security/advisories/GHSA-8cph-rgr4-g5vj", "https://github.com/parse-community/parse-server/pull/10467", "https://github.com/parse-community/parse-server/pull/10468", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-47248", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-8cph-rgr4-g5vj"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/parse-community/parse-server/security-advisories/GHSA-8cph-rgr4-g5vj", "severity": "medium", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/parse-community/parse-server", "summary": "Parse Server's GraphQL "Did you mean ...?" validation suggestions disclose schema to unauthenticated callers", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-12T21:59:50Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-8cph-rgr4-g5vj", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "9.9.1-alpha.2", "package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "parse-server"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 9.0.0, < 9.9.1-alpha.2"}, {"first_patched_version": "8.6.78", "package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "parse-server"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 8.6.78"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: Parse Server's GraphQL "Did you mean ...?" validation suggestions disclose schema to unauthenticated callers
